questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a, b, c, d$ are prime numbers such that $a^2-b^2=c \& d=b+c$, then sum of all the 4 digit numbers formed by using the digits $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}, \mathrm{d}$ without repetition is -
options: an even number divisible by 11
an odd number divisible by 11
questions.solution:
Ans. (B,C)
$\begin{aligned}
& a^2-b^2=c \quad \Rightarrow(a-b)(a+b)=c \\
& \Rightarrow a-b=1 \Rightarrow a=3, b=2, c=5 \& d=7
\end{aligned}$
and sum of all possible numbers
$=3!(1111)(2+3+5+7)=113322$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider all the strings of $n$-digits which can be formed using the numbers from the set $\{0,1,2,3\}$ (may be starting with zero also). If $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{n}}$ denotes the number of strings containing even number of zeros and $\mathrm{O}_n$ denotes the strings containing odd number of zeros then
options: $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{n}}+\mathrm{O}_{\mathrm{n}}=4^{\mathrm{n}}$
$\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{n}}=\frac{4^{\mathrm{n}}+2^{\mathrm{n}}}{2}$
$\mathrm{O}_{\mathrm{n}}=\frac{4^{\mathrm{n}}-2^{\mathrm{n}}}{2}$
$E_n=O_n$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{E}_n=3^n+{ }^n C_2 \cdot 3^{n-2}+{ }^n C_4 \cdot 3^{n-4}+\ldots \ldots \\ & \mathrm{O}_n={ }^n C_1 \cdot 3^{n-1}+{ }^n C_3 \cdot 3^{n-3}+\ldots\end{aligned}$

Adding and subtracting, we get $E_n+O_n=(3+1)^n=4^n, E_n-O_n=(3-1)^2=2^n$
Adding and subtracting again, we get $2 E_n=4^n+2^n \Rightarrow E_n=\frac{4^n+2^n}{2}$
$2 \mathrm{O}_n=4^n-2^n \Rightarrow O_n=\frac{4^n-2^n}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of ways of choosing triplet $(x, y, z)$ such that $z \geq \max \{x, y\}$ and $x, y, z \in\{1,2, \ldots, n, n$ $+1\}$ is
questions.solution:
When $z=n+1$ we can choose $x, y$ from $\{1,2, \ldots, n\}$
$\therefore$ when $\mathrm{z}=\mathrm{n}+1, \mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}$ can be chosen in $\mathrm{n}^2$ ways and $\mathrm{z}=\mathrm{n}, \mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}$ can be chosen in $(\mathrm{n}-1)^2$ ways and so on
$\therefore n^2+(n-1)^2+\ldots+1^2=\frac{1}{6} n(n+1)(2 n+1)$
ways of choosing triplets
Alternatively triplets with $\mathrm{x}=\mathrm{y} \lt \mathrm{z}, \mathrm{x} \lt \mathrm{y} \lt \mathrm{z}, \mathrm{y} \lt \mathrm{x} \lt \mathrm{z}$ can be chosen in ${ }^{n+1} C_2,{ }^{n+1} C_3,{ }^{n+1} C_3$ ways.
There are
${ }^{n+1} C_2+2\left({ }^{n+1} C_3\right)={ }^{n+2} C_2+{ }^{n+1} C_3=2\left({ }^{n+2} C_3\right)-{ }^{n+1} C_2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Positive integers are written on all the faces of a cube, one on each. At each corner (vertex) of the cube, the product of the numbers on the faces that meet at the corner is written. The sum of the numbers written at all the comer is 2004. If $T$ denotes the sum of the number on all the faces, the possible value of $T$ are
questions.solution:
Let $A B C D P Q R S$ be a cube, the numbers $a, b, c, d, e, f$ be written on the faces
$\Rightarrow$ ade $+a b e+a b f+a d f+c d e+b c e+b c f+c d f=2004$
$\Rightarrow(e+f)(a+c)(b+d)=2^2 \cdot 3 \cdot 167$

Possible combination are $4 \cdot 3 \cdot 167,2 \cdot 6 \cdot 167,2 \cdot 3 \cdot 334,2 \cdot 2 \cdot 501$
$T=a+b+c+d+e+f=174,175,339$, or 505
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\mathrm{H}_{\text {, }}$ denotes the number of ways in which n horses participating in a race can reach the finishing line. when one or more horses can reaci the finishing line at the same time. Then
options: $\mathrm{H}_2=3$
$\mathrm{H}_3=13$
$\mathrm{H}_4=75$
$\mathrm{H}_3=10$
questions.solution:
The sequence of two horses reaching the finishing line can be 2 or $1+1$ $\Rightarrow$ Number of ways $=1+2!=3$
The sequence of three horses reaching the finishing line and number of ways are shown below
$3 \Rightarrow 1$ way
$2+1 \Rightarrow{ }^3 C_2=3$ ways
$1+2 \Rightarrow{ }^3 C_1=3$ ways
$1+1+1$
$\Rightarrow 3!=6$
Hence. $\mathrm{H}_3=1+3+3+6=13$
The sequence of four horses reading the finishing line and number of ways are shown below
$\begin{aligned} & 4 \Rightarrow 1 \text { ways } \\ & 3+1 \Rightarrow{ }^4 \mathrm{C}_3 \text { ways } \\ & 1+3 \Rightarrow{ }^4 \mathrm{C}_1 \text { ways } \\ & 2+2 \Rightarrow{ }^4 \mathrm{C}_2 \\ & 2+1+1\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{4!}{2!1!1!2!}\right) \times 3!$ ways
$1+1+1+1$
$\Rightarrow 4!$ ways
Hence. $\mathrm{H}_4=1+4+4+6+36+24=75$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Number of natural numbers between 100 and 1000 which can be formed using the digits $1,2,3,4,5,6$, 7 if each digit is used at most once, is also equal to
options: the number of triangles that can be formed with 12 points in a plane with exactly 5 of them being collinear
the number of ways in which 4 alike apples can be distributed among 10 children if each child gets at most one apple
the number of ways of distributing 16 identical fruits among 5 children if each child gets not less than 2 fruits
questions.solution:
Number of natural numbers between 100 and 1000 which can be formed with the digits $1,2,3,4,5,6$, 7 if each digit is used atmost once $=7 \times 6 \times 5=210$
7 if each digit is used atmost once $=7 \times 6 \times 5=210$
Option (B) $={ }^{12} \mathrm{C}_3-{ }^{-5} \mathrm{C}_3=210$
Option $(\mathrm{C})={ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_4 \times 1=210$
Option (D) $={ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_4=210$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

options: Exponent of 11 in 2009 ! is 199
The total number of triangles that can be formed by joining any 3 points, from vertices and the centre of a regular octagon are 126

The number of quadrilaterals that can be constructed by joining any four vertices of a dodecagon (polygon with 12 sides) such that none of the side of the quadrilateral is side of the given dodecagon are 105
The total number of different selections of at least one letter from IITJEE are 35

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If words are formed using all the letters of the word 'CHITRANJEEVI', then:
options: number of words in which all vowels are separated is $7 ! \times{ }^8 C_5 \times \frac{5 !}{2 ! 2 !}$
number of words in which vowels appear in alphabetical order is $\frac{12 !}{5 !}$
number of words which contains the word 'CHITRA' is $\frac{7 !}{2 !}$
number of words which contains the word 'IITJEE' is 7 !
questions.solution:
CHTRNJV (II) (EE), A
(1) $\begin{array}{r}|-|-|-|-|-|-|-| \\ 7 ! \times{ }^8 C_5 \times \frac{5 !}{2 ! \cdot 2 !}\end{array}$
(2) $\frac{12 !}{5 !}$
(3) $\frac{7 !}{2 !}$ (CHITRA) N, J, V, I, (E, E)
(4) 7! (IITJEE)  < C, H, R, N, V, A.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x y z=2^3 \times 3^1 \times 5^2 \times 7^1$, then identify which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: If $x, y, z \in N$, then number of ordered triplets $(x, y, z)$ is 540 .
If $x, y, z \in I$, then number of ordered triplets $(x, y, z)$ is 1620 .
If $P=x y z$, then number of divisors of $P$ which are divisible by 12 is 12 .
If $P=x y z$, then product of divisors of $P$ which are divisible by 12 is $(12 P)^6$.
questions.solution:
(1) ${ }^5 C_2 \times{ }^3 C_2 \times{ }^4 C_2 \times{ }^3 C_2=540$
(2) ${ }^5 C_2 \times{ }^3 C_2 \times{ }^4 C_2 \times{ }^3 C_2 \times 4=2160$
(3) $P=2^3 \times 3^1 \times 5^2 \times 7^1$
$\therefore \quad$ Number of divisors of $P$ which are divisible by $12=2 \times 3 \times 2=12$
(4) $\frac{P}{12}=2^1 \times 5^2 \times 7^1$
Product of divisors of $P$ which are divisible by $12=(12)^{12}\left(\frac{P}{12}\right)^6=(12 P)^6$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: All the 7 digit numbers containing each of the digits $1,2,3,4,5,6,7$ exactly once and not divisible by 5 are arranged in the increasing order. Then -
options: $1800^{\text {th }}$ number in the list is 3124567
$1897^{\text {th }}$ number in the list is 4213567
$1994^{\text {th }}$ number in the list is 4312567
$2001^{\text {th }}$ number in the list is 4315726
questions.solution:
Total numbers which are not divisible by 5 are $=6!\times 6=4320$
Now when 1 or 2 or 3 occupies the $7^{\text {th }}$ place, then the number of numbers $=3 \times 5!\times 5=1800$ (last can be filled only in 5 ways) $\therefore 1800^{\text {th }}$ number in the list is 3765421 . when $1^{\text {st }}$ two places are 41 $\qquad$ then
number of numbers $=4!\times 4=96$ $\therefore 1897^{\text {th }}$ number in the list is 4213567 . with 42 $\qquad$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { number of numbers }=4!\times 4=96 \\ & \text { Total so far }=1800+192=1992 . \end{aligned}$ $\therefore 1994^{\text {th }}$ number in the list is 4312576 . $1^{\text {st }}$ three places are filled as $4312 \ldots \ldots . .$.
$\begin{aligned} & \text { number of numbers }=2!\times 2=4 \\ & {[\text { Total }=1992+4=1996]} \end{aligned}$ Now, when first 4 places are, $4315 \times \times \times$ then the remaining 3 places in each case be filled in $3!=6$ ways which makes total numbers $=2002$ and the $(2002)^{\mathrm{th}}$ number is 4315762 Hence $(2001)^{\text {st }}$ number is just before it $=4315726$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A contest consisting of ranking 10 songs of which 6 are Indian classic and 4 are western songs. Number of ways of ranking so that:
options: there are exactly 3 indian classic songs in top 5 is $(5 !)^3$
top rank goes to indian classic song is 6.9 !
the ranks of all western songs are consecutive is $4 ! 7$ !
the 6 indian classic songs are in a specified order is ${ }^{10} P_4$
questions.solution:
(a) Number of ways $={ }^6 C_3 \times{ }^4 C_2 \times 5 ! \times 5 !=(5 !)^3$
(b) Number of ways $={ }^6 C_1 \times 9 !=6 \times 9$ !
(c) Number of ways $=(6+1) ! \times 4 !=7 ! 4$ !
(d) Number of ways $={ }^{10} C_6 \times 1 \times 4 !={ }^{10} C_4 \times 4 !={ }^{10} P_4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Coefficient of $x^2 y^3 z^4$ in the expansion of $(x+y+z)^9$ is equal to
options: The number of ways in which 9 things of which 2 alike of one kind, 3 alike of $2^{\text {nd }}$ kind, and 4 alike of $3^{\text {rd }}$ kind can be arranged.
The number of ways in which 9 identical things can be distributed in 3 persons each receiving atleast two things.
The number of ways in which 9 identical things can be distributed in 3 persons each receiving none one or more.
The number of ways in which 9 different books can be tied up in to three bundles one containing 2 , other 3 and third containing 4 books.
questions.solution:
Consider $(x+y+z)^9={ }^9 C_r x^{9-r}(y+z)^r={ }^9 C_r x^{9-r} \cdot r^r C_p y^{r-p} z^p[x+(y+z)]^9$ put $\mathrm{r}=7$; $\mathrm{p}=4$
or ${ }^9 C_2 \cdot{ }^7 C_3 \cdot{ }^4 C_4=\frac{9!}{2!3!4!}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $S=\{14,15,16, \ldots \ldots, 22\}$. If $N$ is the number of subsets of $S$ containing two or more elements such that sum of the least and greatest elements has odd number of divisors, then:
options: the number of prime factors of $N$ is 3
the number of divisors of $N$ is 8
sum of the digits of $N$ is 8
the number of divisors of $N$ is 16
questions.solution:
$S=\{14,15,16, \ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots, 22\}$
Sum of the least and greatest number must be a perfect square i.e., 36
( $\because$ number of divisors of their sum is odd.)
(i) $14, \ldots \ldots \ldots, 22 \rightarrow 2^7=128$
(ii) $15, \ldots \ldots \ldots, 21 \rightarrow 2^5=032$
(iii) $16, \ldots \ldots \ldots, 20 \rightarrow 2^3=008$
(iv) $17, \ldots \ldots . ., 19 \rightarrow 2^1=\frac{002}{170}$
$N=170=2 \times 5 \times 17$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If n lines are drawn in a plane such that ne two are parallel and no three are concurrent then the number of regions the plane is divided is denoted by $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{o}}$ then
options: $\mathrm{P}=29$
questions.solution:
Blank plane is one region
Every line adds are region to the plane
Every point of intersection in plane adds one-more region
Hence, $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{n}}=1+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_2+\mathrm{n}=\frac{\mathrm{n}^2+\mathrm{n}+2}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
